mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent, it signals a labor market cooling that historically precedes downward earnings revisions, depressing equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production rises while housing starts fall indicates a supply-demand imbalance that compresses corporate margins, leading to negative equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term constructed from INDPRO and HOUST shows no predictive power for forward returns in out-of-sample testing, or the sign of the coefficient flips consistently across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 250, rising real activity forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which historically suppresses equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not decline when GDPC1 rises during CPIAUCSL periods above 250, or the predictive R-squared drops below 0.01 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns are not significantly positive when PAYEMS growth exceeds 150 thousand.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer credit outstanding contracts for two consecutive months, it signals a credit crunch that restricts household spending, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline following consecutive RSAFS drops, or the signal hit rate falls below 45 percent in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in industrial production and federal funds rate indicates supply-driven growth meeting tightening liquidity, which historically compresses equity multiples and yields negative returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint movement of INDPRO and FEDFUNDS fails to predict forward returns with statistical significance, or the interaction effect reverses sign in more than 50 percent of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery phase, a rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that drive wage growth and consumer spending, historically boosting equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not increase when UNRATE declines during recovery periods, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.
